purpose: Causal-closure arm: identical NIM feedback protocol as safe_panda_core_nim_feedback_v1, but on the soft-slack base. Predeclared hypothesis: the harm measured in the NIM arm (CS2 -0.24 vs tube, collisions 24->77) is caused by the infeasibility-freeze mechanism (hard CBF + fail-closed zero command); the L1 slack valve removes that mechanism, so on this base the same gamma tightening should not inflate collisions and should be neutral or better versus the soft-slack fixed arm.

provider.name: nvidia_nim
provider.request_policy: checkpoint pre-seeded from the nim_feedback_v1 arm; the identical prompt must replay from cache, so the expected number of new API requests is zero
provider.replay_rule: every request/response checkpointed verbatim before use; reruns replay from checkpoints

feedback_protocol.user_utterance: Watch out! I think it's going to crash soon.
feedback_protocol.utterance_source: paper Table-4 ablation protocol, Section V-C
feedback_protocol.context_given_to_llm: current gamma (0.15), task instruction, scene summary, per paper eq. (16) OF inputs
feedback_protocol.invalid_output_policy: unparseable or out-of-range outputs are recorded and the episode keeps gamma 0.15

claim_limits: simulator-only, EE-reach-only, NIM llama-3.1-8b-instruct as GPT-4o substitute
descriptive; single deterministic gamma decision (temperature 0), not per-episode adaptation
